Beaujolais Blanc Chardonnay

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Terres Dorées' Chardonnay from the golden limestone of Pierres Dorées: between Mâcon and Chablis in character, with peach-blossom freshness, chalky mineral and restrained richness.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais L'Ancien

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Elegant

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Jean-Paul Brun's signature Beaujolais AOC uses old-vine, whole-cluster Gamay from the golden limestone soils of Pierres Dorées for a richer, more textured and age-worthy expression than typical Beaujolais.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais-Villages Domaine du Comte

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Domaine Karim VionnetBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Karim Vionnet's estate Beaujolais-Villages from the Domaine du Comte parcels: darker-fruited and more structured than a typical Villages, showing the producer's Morgon-influenced approach to low-intervention Gamay.

Cuvée page →

Beaujolais-Villages Esprit d'Une Vie

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Domaine Karim VionnetBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Vionnet's prestige Villages cuvée from older parcels: Morgon-influenced depth and structure, with kirsch, incense and a long iron-mineral finish that punches above appellation level.

Cuvée page →

Brouilly

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Château de la Chaize's estate Brouilly from its historic 220-hectare Odenas property: full-flavoured, traditionally structured Gamay with ripe red fruit and reliable house style.

Brouilly

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Maison Georges DuboeufBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Duboeuf's Brouilly from selected Mont Brouilly-area estates: accessible, medium-bodied and fruit-forward with the appellation's characteristic fresh cherry and clean mineral profile.

Brouilly Cuvée Alma Mater

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Domaine Jean-Claude LapaluBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Lapalu's cellar-selection Brouilly: deeper and more structured than the Vieilles Vignes, with darker fruit, herbal complexity and a savoury, mineral finish.

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Contemplative

Château de la ChaizeBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Château de la Chaize's mid-tier Brouilly, longer-aged and more structured than the classique, with ripe fruit concentration and earthy Brouilly depth.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Funky

Domaine Jean-Claude LapaluBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Old-vine Brouilly from a natural-wine pioneer: sauvage cherry and iron mineral character with the energy of whole-cluster fermentation.

Chenas

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Bold · Rustic

Domaine des BureauxBeaujolais€€€€€€Still Red

Chenas is the smallest and least-known Beaujolais cru. Domaine des Bureaux produces a concentrated, spicy Gamay that resembles Moulin-a-Vent in structure.

Domaine Émile CheyssonBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Cheysson's parcel-selection Cuvée Prestige from older vines at Chiroubles' coolest high-altitude sites: deeper fruit concentration, firmer structure and genuine ageing potential.

Chiroubles La Pas de la Mule

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Domaine Émile CheyssonBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Named for the old mule path across the Chiroubles hillside, this single-parcel cuvée is Cheysson's most mineral and age-worthy wine: granite-driven, long-finishing Gamay at its most serious.

Voûte des Crozes' Côte de Brouilly from the volcanic Mont Brouilly slopes: dark-cherry and stony-mineral profile with the appellation's characteristic volcanic lift.

Terres Dorées' Côte de Brouilly from Mont Brouilly: volcanic-mineral depth with dark cherry concentration and the firm, structured grip of the appellation's blue-syenite soils.

Thivin's classic Côte de Brouilly blending seven parcels across the hill for a precise, floral Gamay with stony lift and refreshing acidity.

Domaine Richard RottiersBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Roussette

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Domaine Terres Dorées (Jean-Paul Brun)Beaujolais€€€€€€€Still White

Named for the golden-limestone colour of the Pierres Dorées hillsides, Roussette is Terres Dorées' richest white: beeswax, ripe peach and hazelnut character from old-barrel Chardonnay on the golden stone terroir.

Cuvée page →

Régnié Exsultet

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Rustic

Domaine Christian DucrouxBeaujolais€€€€€€€Still Red

Ducroux's prestige Régnié from Demeter-certified biodynamic farming: deep cherry-and-clay concentration, wild-yeast complexity and firm mineral structure.

Cuvée page →

Aile d'Argent

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Mouton RothschildBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still White

White wine of Mouton Rothschild from 7 hectares of sandy-gravelly soils, reviving a historic Medoc tradition. Rich and complex; Sauvignon Blanc provides drive while Semillon adds texture; partial barrel fermentation contributes subtle vanilla without overwhelming fruit.

Cuvée page →

Aromes de Pavie

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau PavieBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Originally the second wine of Pavie, now an independent brand since 2018 sourced from a dedicated 11-hectare east-plateau parcel. Merlot-Cabernet Franc blend of medium body with dark cherry, espresso and salty-mineral character; approachable on release.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still White

Single-plot Semillon from 75-year-old vines in a three-hectare block; the house premium white. Dry and elegant with the waxy, textured Semillon character that recalls white Pessac-Leognan at a fraction of the price. Anne Krebiehl MW-praised.

Cuvée page →

Bauduc Les Trois Hectares Rouge

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's premium red from a single Bordeaux Superieur block: 75% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Sauvignon aged in small barrels for added structure. Anne Krebiehl MW describes it as juicy, plush, and beautifully open with oak-kissed fruit.

Cuvée page →

Blanc de Lynch-Bages

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still White

Historically one of the first white wines produced by a Grand Cru Classe in the Medoc. Primarily Sauvignon Blanc driven with Semillon body; partially barrel-fermented but retains freshness through tank-vinified component.

Cuvée page →

Chapelle d'Ausone

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau AusoneBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Ausone, sourced from the youngest Cabernet Franc and Merlot vines on the same limestone hillside. Crisp, elegant and unmistakably mineral, with a fruity register that opens earlier than the grand vin.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Bauduc Bordeaux Superieur

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau BauducBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

Bauduc's entry-level claret; a straightforward Merlot-Cabernet blend offering reliable, juicy red-fruit character and soft tannins at an accessible price. Drink now or hold for up to seven years. The wine is consistently good for its level.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Haut-Lariveau

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau Moulin Pey-LabrieBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

100% Merlot Fronsac from the 4.5-hectare Haut-Lariveau parcel acquired by the Hubau family in 1990. More approachable and fruit-forward than its Canon-Fronsac sibling, farmed with the same biodynamic discipline.

Cuvée page →

Chateau Moulin Pey-Labrie

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Chateau Moulin Pey-LabrieBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The benchmark Canon-Fronsac red from 6.75 hectares of 65-year-average-age vines, farmed biodynamically and organically. Merlot with 5% Malbec produces a fragrant, perfumed wine with remarkable freshness for the Right Bank.

Cuvée page →

Chateau de la Riviere

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The flagship Fronsac estate red from 60 hectares on clay-limestone soils above the Dordogne. Merlot-led and supple with dark chocolate and ripe cherry; aged in spectacular 16th-century underground cellars at constant 13 degrees.

Cuvée page →

Château Larose-Trintaudon

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The largest single vineyard in the Médoc produces an consistently approachable, fruit-forward Haut-Médoc with a well-balanced structure and excellent everyday drinkability at an accessible price.

Cuvée page →

Château de Camensac

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Château de CamensacBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

A revived Fifth Growth finding its stride under the Merlaut family: Cabernet-forward Haut-Médoc structure with dark fruit, cedar and characteristic menthol freshness from deep gravel soils near Saint-Julien.

Cuvée page →

Cos d'Estournel Blanc

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Chateau Cos d'EstournelBordeaux$$$$$$$$$Still White

One of the most prestigious white Bordeaux wines from Saint-Estephe, produced from a 10-hectare dedicated white wine parcel. Fresh, vibrant, and barrel-fermented; the Sauvignon Blanc drive and Semillon texture achieve remarkable complexity.

Cuvée page →

Croix Canon

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Chateau CanonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Canon (formerly Clos Canon, rebranded Croix Canon from the 2012 vintage). Elegant, medium-bodied with mint, cherry and floral character; structured enough for 10+ years but designed for earlier pleasure.

Cuvée page →

Echo de Lynch-Bages

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Lynch-BagesBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Lynch-Bages since 2007 (previously sold as Chateau Haut-Bages Averous). Higher Merlot component than the grand vin provides accessible fruit and earlier drinking; retains characteristic Lynch-Bages tobacco and cassis signature.

Cuvée page →

Fleur de Clinet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau ClinetBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Clinet from estate vines, made to reveal its qualities early. Smooth-textured, fruit-driven Pomerol Merlot with approachable tannins and genuine ageing potential without requiring long cellaring.

Cuvée page →

L'Héritage de Chasse-Spleen

3.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The Haut-Médoc AOC second-level wine of Chasse-Spleen, made from satellite vineyard parcels. Easy-drinking, herb-inflected red fruit for everyday occasions.

Cuvée page →

L'Oratoire de Chasse-Spleen

3.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Château Chasse-SpleenBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The approachable second wine of Chasse-Spleen from the Moulis AOC: Merlot-driven, with enticing red fruit and soft tannins, ready to enjoy without extended cellaring.

Cuvée page →

La Chapelle de La Mission Haut-Brion

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Savoury · Elegant

Chateau La Mission Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of La Mission Haut-Brion, incorporating grapes from younger vines and since 2006 from the former La Tour Haut-Brion vineyard. Shares the Talence gravel terroir; accessible dark fruit with cedar and spice, drinkable earlier than the grand vin.

Cuvée page →

La Closerie de Camensac

3.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing

Château de CamensacBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Château Camensac, prioritising freshness and supple tannins from younger vine selections. Light and immediately appealing with characteristic Haut-Médoc menthol freshness.

La Demoiselle de Sociando-Mallet

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Sociando-MalletBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The Merlot-dominant second wine of Sociando-Mallet, offering earlier approachability with a more typically Médoc fruit profile than the austere and structured grand vin.

Cuvée page →

La Gravette de Certan

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Vieux Chateau CertanBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible, Merlot-dominant second selection from Vieux Chateau Certan, made from younger vines and less-selected barrels. Forward, fruit-driven, and approachable early but retaining the estate's hallmark iron-mineral signature.

Cuvée page →

La Réserve de Léoville Barton

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Léoville BartonBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

An elegant and approachable expression of Saint-Julien from younger vines, with a higher Merlot proportion giving early accessibility and soft ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Le Blanc de Chateau de la Riviere

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau de la RiviereBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

An unusual Bordeaux Blanc built on Sauvignon Gris rather than Semillon, giving tropical and citrus character with a rich, textured palate. Only 5,600 bottles produced annually; fermented mostly in stainless steel to preserve freshness.

Cuvée page →

Le Carillon de l'Angelus

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau AngelusBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Angelus, Merlot-dominant with a forward, polished character. Bright red fruit, soft spice and floral tones make it approachable younger than the grand vin while retaining the estate's Merlot generosity.

Cuvée page →

Le Clarence de Haut-Brion

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Chateau Haut-BrionBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red of Haut-Brion, renamed from Bahans Haut-Brion in 2007. Merlot-dominated in most vintages with the same Pessac terroir as the grand vin, but more approachable in youth, showing red plum, blueberry, and mineral freshness.

Cuvée page →

Le Clementin du Pape Clement Rouge

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pape ClementBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Pape Clement since 1986. Merlot-dominant for approachability; round red cherry and currant fruit with cedar and soft spice notes. Designed for earlier drinking than the grand vin while reflecting the same Pessac terroir.

Cuvée page →

Le Lys de Lafaurie-Peyraguey

3.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Lafaurie-PeyragueyBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

A dry white Bordeaux introduced in 2014 from the Lafaurie-Peyraguey estate in Bommes. Sauvignon Blanc-dominant with Semillon adding body; white flowers, lemon blossom, and fresh citrus lead a mineral, herb-tinged palate. An accessible entry into the Sauternes estate's terroir as a dry expression.

Cuvée page →

Le Pauillac de Chateau Latour

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau LatourBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Third wine of Latour produced since 1989. Merlot-dominant and made primarily from young vines; developed for the restaurant market as a generously fruity, earlier-drinking Pauillac.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Cheval

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Elegant · Easygoing

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Cheval Blanc: same terroir and typicity as the grand vin but in a more accessible, forward style. Charming black-fruit and cedar profile, silky tannins, and an earlier drinking window.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Cheval Blanc

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

Chateau Cheval BlancBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

Barrel-fermented Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from Cheval Blanc's estate, classified as Bordeaux Blanc since Saint-Emilion AOC prohibits white varieties. Elegant citrus and flint profile with saline length.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Blanc

3.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still White

The second white wine of Smith Haut Lafitte. Sauvignon Blanc-driven with Semillon adding roundness; lime tree, verbena, and citrus oil aromas lead a fresh, floral palate with elderflower and white peach. Vibrant acidity makes it an excellent aperitif and shellfish partner.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Haut Lafitte Rouge

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Savoury

Chateau Smith Haut LafitteBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second red wine of Smith Haut Lafitte, sharing the estate's Martillac gravel terroir. A Cabernet-Sauvignon-led blend with cassis, fresh herbs, and pencil-shaving notes in an approachable medium-bodied style suitable for earlier drinking.

Cuvée page →

Le Petit Lion du Marquis de Las Cases

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Château Léoville-Las CasesBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The official second wine of Léoville-Las Cases since 2007, Merlot-dominant for earlier accessibility while retaining the Saint-Julien precision of the parent estate.

Cuvée page →

Les Hauts de Pontet-Canet

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Pontet-CanetBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Pontet-Canet since 1982. Produced in limited quantities as Alfred Tesseron prefers lower yields and declassification over large second-wine production; earlier-drinking, approachable Pauillac from biodynamic-certified vineyards.

Les Hauts de Trintaudon

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Château Larose-TrintaudonBordeaux€€€€€€Still Red

The accessible second wine of Larose-Trintaudon, sourced from the same vines and terroir as the grand vin but crafted for early approachability and everyday enjoyment.

Cuvée page →

Les Pensees de Lafleur

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Chateau LafleurBordeaux€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Produced from a separate 0.8-hectare crescent parcel with deeper soils, Les Pensees is not a second wine but a distinct expression of the Lafleur terroir. More Merlot-forward than the grand vin, with the same biodynamic farming and iron-mineral character.

Mondot

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau Troplong MondotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

Second wine of Troplong Mondot, Merlot-dominant with emphasis on freshness and early drinkability. A juicy, earthy black cherry and spring flower profile with wet-stone limestone terroir character; approachable and unpretentious.

Cuvée page →

Pavillon Blanc du Château Margaux

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Château MargauxBordeaux€€€€€€€€€€Still White

A rare Sauvignon Blanc white Bordeaux of extraordinary mineral precision: gunsmoke, slate and saline drive a taut citrus frame that ages beautifully over a decade or more.

Cuvée page →

Petit Becot

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

Chateau Beau-Sejour BecotBordeaux€€€€€€€Still Red

The renamed second wine of Beau-Sejour Becot (formerly Tournelle de Beau-Sejour Becot), renamed Petit Becot in 2012. Merlot-dominant with fresh red cherry character and gentle tannin; designed for early drinking pleasure.

Cuvée page →

Petit-Figeac

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Easygoing

Chateau FigeacBordeaux€€€€€€€€Still Red

The second wine of Chateau Figeac, one of the oldest second wines on the Right Bank (first produced 1945). Shares the same Cabernet-influenced gravel terroir and blending trio as the grand vin but from younger vines; lighter, more refined and more accessible earlier.

Cuvée page →

Prieur de Meyney

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Chateau MeyneyBordeaux$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Chateau Meyney since 1979, named for the estate's 17th-century priory origins. Produced from younger vines (approximately 35-40 years) using minimal new oak to preserve fresh fruit character; a reliable everyday Saint-Estephe.

Cuvée page →

R de Rieussec

3.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Chateau RieussecBordeaux€€€€€€€Still White

The dry white wine of Chateau Rieussec, a relative newcomer first produced in 1999. A fresh, citrussy Sauvignon Blanc and Semillon blend from the Fargues-de-Langon terroir with herbal and mineral freshness; lighter-styled than the Sauternes grand vin but sharing its Semillon richness.

Reserve de la Comtesse

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Elegant

Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de LalandeBordeaux$$$$$$$$Still Red

Second wine of Pichon Lalande since 1973. Described by the estate as a true Pauillac brand in its own right. Higher Petit Verdot proportion adds structure and spice; the estate's characteristic silk texture is detectable even at this level.

Dow's PortDouro Valley€€€€€€€€Fortified Port

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Fruity

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€Still Red

A benchmark Vajra Dolcetto, juicy and purple-fruited with the soft tannin and easy drinkability that define the everyday red of the Langhe.

Cuvée page →

G.D. Vajra Langhe Freisa Kye

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still Red

Kye is Vajra's serious, oak-aged take on Freisa, a near-forgotten Nebbiolo relative, giving a tannic, high-toned red with wild-berry character.

G.D. Vajra Langhe Nebbiolo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€€Still Red

Vajra's Langhe Nebbiolo is a fresh, fragrant and early-drinking introduction to the grape, drawn from younger vines around Vergne.

G.D. Vajra Langhe Riesling Petracine

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Elegant

G.D. VajraPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still White

A rare Langhe Riesling and a pioneering Vajra project, Petracine is a dry, mineral, high-acid white from sandstone soils in the high hills of Barolo.

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

La ScolcaPiedmont€€€€€€€€Still White

The Black Label flagship that defined premium Gavi, a sophisticated 100% Cortese of elegant structure, freshness and intense aromatic complexity that ages remarkably well.

★★★★★★★★★★

Matteo CorreggiaPiedmont€€€€€€Still White

A reference Roero Arneis from a house that championed the grape, a fresh, dry white expressing Arneis as the Roero's signature variety.

PeccheninoPiedmont€€€€€€Still Red

A red-purple Dogliani with violet nuances, blackberry, raspberry, blueberry and red cherry with mineral notes, medium-bodied with soft tannins, moderate acidity and good balance.

★★★★★★★★★★

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€Still White

A barrel-aged white Rioja with cult status: deeply oxidative, nutty and waxy, with piercing acidity that keeps it electric for decades, often compared to white Burgundy and old Hunter Semillon.

★★★★★★★★★★

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Released only in great years and only after a decade-plus in the cellar; ethereal, savoury and impossibly fresh, the Gran Reserva is one of the wine world's great old-style reds.

R. Lopez de Heredia Vina TondoniaRioja€€€€€€€€Still Red

The reference traditional Rioja Reserva: pale brick colour, oxidative dried-fruit and balsamic aromatics over a fine-boned, high-acid frame that drinks like old Burgundy.

The Pietrarizzo contrada on Etna's north slope at 800 metres yields Benanti's most mineral and savoury Nerello Mascalese: a wine of austere mineral precision and remarkable ageing potential from pre-phylloxera vine material.

Benanti Rovittello

Benanti ViticoltoriSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

From Rovittello on the north slope, this Benanti red shows the classic Etna paradox: light ruby colour but profound mineral depth, firm tannins and soaring acidity demanding patient cellaring.

Benanti's top-tier Etna Rosso from the Serra della Contessa contrada at 700 metres: concentrated, structured, and among the most age-worthy expressions of Nerello Mascalese on the volcano.

★★★★★★★★★★

COS PithosSicily€€€€€€€Orange

Nero d'Avola fermented and aged in terracotta amphorae without oak: the clay vessel imparts earthy minerality and textural grip unique among Sicilian reds.

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still White

Anthilia is Donnafugata's flagship entry white: Catarratto and Grecanico from the Contessa Entellina estate in a fresh, floral and mineral style that has defined quality Sicilian white wine for three decades.

Kabir is Donnafugata's lighter Pantelleria sweet wine: fresher and less concentrated than Ben Rye, from Zibibbo grapes dried for a shorter period, producing a floral, juicy dessert wine of great charm.

Donnafugata Lighea

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Off Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Fruity

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€Still White

Lighea blends Zibibbo's heady Muscat-like perfume with native white grapes in a delicately off-dry aromatic style: floral, peachy, with balancing acidity that keeps it from cloying.

Cuvée page →

Donnafugata Vigna di Gabri

★★★★★★★★★★

DonnafugataSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Vigna di Gabri is Donnafugata's premium Contessa Entellina white: a blend of native Sicilian whites with Chardonnay, showing greater textural complexity than Anthilia with a more persistent mineral finish.

Feudi del Pisciotto's DOCG expression from the restored manor estate in the Val di Noto shows the Cerasuolo di Vittoria character cleanly: cherry, mineral and herbaceous with the appellation's characteristic clay-soil earthiness.

Feudi del PisciottoSicily€€€€€€Still White

Feudi del Pisciotto's varietal Grillo from the Val di Noto estate delivers the grape's characteristic citrus-blossom and sea-mineral freshness from a warm inland site moderated by its position on the edge of the Sughereta reserve.

Feudo MontoniSicily€€€€€€Still White

Grillo grown at altitude in central Sicily develops an unusual aromatic tension; this steel-only white is crisper and more mineral than coastal Grillos with notable herbal lift.

Ripa di Moraci is Cavanera's prestige Etna Rosso from the Verzella contrada: a firm-structured, mineral Nerello showcasing the quality achievable at the Firriato Etna estate.

★★★★★★★★★★

FirriatoSicily€€€€€€Still White

Firriato's entry white blends the native almond-citrus character of Catarratto with Chardonnay's stone-fruit roundness in a clean, everyday Sicilian white from the Trapani estate.

Frank Cornelissen Magma

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Funky

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Magma is one of Italy's most sought-after cult wines: pure Nerello Mascalese from ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines above 1,000 metres, no sulphur added, aged in amphora. The wine embodies volcanic terroir at its most extreme and unfiltered.

Cuvée page →

Frank Cornelissen MunJebel Bianco

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Funky · Contemplative

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€Still White

MunJebel Bianco from Cornelissen shows the volcanic-herbal dimension of Etna whites when made without additions: textured, complex and with a distinctively wild herbal-mineral character from the blend of Carricante, Catarratto and ancient Minella.

Cuvée page →

Frank Cornelissen MunJebel Rosso

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Funky · Easygoing

Frank CornelissenSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

MunJebel Rosso is the accessible entry into the Cornelissen range: zero-sulphur Nerello Mascalese from multiple contrade, vibrant and mineral, embodying the Etna natural-wine philosophy in an everyday format.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo 'A Rina

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The prestige Russo single-vineyard: 'A Rina (dialect for 'la rena' - the sand) from some of the deepest sandy volcanic soils on the north slope, producing the most austere and age-demanding wine in the portfolio.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo Feudo

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Savoury

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Feudo is the most structured of the Russo single-contrada range: darker-fruited, firmer in tannin, with a chalky mineral depth that demands patience but rewards handsomely.

Cuvée page →

Girolamo Russo San Lorenzo

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Girolamo RussoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

San Lorenzo is the most immediately accessible of the Russo single-contrada range: fragrant, iron-streaked, with vibrant sour cherry and a saline volcanic freshness that makes it the most drinkable Russo Etna Rosso in its youth.

Cuvée page →

Graci Arcuria

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

GraciSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Arcuria is the Graci flagship: from 52 acres of ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines on the north slope, it epitomises Etna's Burgundian paradox - light ruby colour belying deep mineral complexity and firm, precise tannins.

Cuvée page →

Graci Barbabecchi

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

GraciSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

From the Barbabecchi contrada at higher elevation than Arcuria, this Graci red is the most structured and demanding of the range: dense, chalky, and extraordinarily long-lived.

Cuvée page →

Graci Etna Rosso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

GraciSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The entry Graci Etna Rosso offers immediate pleasure: Nerello Mascalese at its most approachable - fragrant, herb-scented, with the estate's characteristic saline mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Hauner Malvasia delle Lipari Naturale

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Medium Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Hauner (Malvasia delle Lipari)Sicily€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

The Naturale style is a lighter, fresher take on Malvasia delle Lipari: less concentrated than the Passito but showing the grape's characteristic floral-apricot aromatics and the volcanic salinity of the Aeolian Islands in a more immediately approachable register.

Cuvée page →

Maggio Vini Moscato di Noto

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Festive · Romantic

Maggio ViniSicily€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

Maggio Vini's Moscato di Noto is a delicately sweet fortified-adjacent late-harvest Moscato Bianco from the sun-drenched Noto terroir: peach blossom, orange and apricot with balancing acidity from the variety's natural freshness.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Grillo

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Mandrarossa's coastal Grillo from Menfi shows the grape's Mediterranean character: sun-warmed citrus blossom and peach with sea-salt mineral and high acidity from close proximity to the Tyrrhenian coast.

Cuvée page →

Mandrarossa Terre di Giumara Fiano

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Elegant

Settesoli MandrarossaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Mandrarossa's single-vineyard Fiano from the Terre di Giumara estate shows the variety's characteristic nutty-mineral quality from Menfi's calcareous-clay soils close to the Mediterranean coast.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Grappoli del Grillo

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Marco De BartoliSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Grappoli del Grillo is De Bartoli's dry still table wine from Grillo, made without oxidation: a fresh, mineral white that showcases the grape's natural salinity, citrus character and almond-bitter finish when vinified as a conventional still wine rather than a perpetuo.

Cuvée page →

Marco De Bartoli Pietra Pumice

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Funky · Contemplative

Marco De Bartoli - Bukkuram (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Pietra Pumice (pumice stone) is De Bartoli's dry Zibibbo table wine from Pantelleria: made without additions on the volcanic pumice soils, it shows the wild mineral character of the island when Zibibbo is vinified dry and naturally.

Cuvée page →

Murgo Etna Rosso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

MurgoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Murgo's estate Etna Rosso from the eastern slope of Etna: approachable and herb-scented, with the saline freshness characteristic of the Santa Venerina area.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti Bombolieri

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Occhipinti's top-tier DOCG from the Bombolieri single vineyard: equal parts Frappato and Nero d'Avola in their most complex and age-worthy expression, mineral-driven with remarkable persistence.

Cuvée page →

Occhipinti SP68 Bianco

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

Arianna OcchipintiSicily€€€€€€€Still White

The white counterpart to SP68 Rosso: Grecanico and Zibibbo from volcanic soils, aromatically intense, mineral and saline with a citrus-peach freshness.

Cuvée page →

Palmento Costanzo Di Me

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Palmento CostanzoSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Di Me (meaning 'of me' or 'for me') is Palmento Costanzo's prestige expression: single-vineyard Nerello Mascalese from the oldest vines in the Santo Spirito contrada, displaying the estate's most complex mineral and structural character.

Cuvée page →

Palmento Costanzo Mofete Etna Rosso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Palmento CostanzoSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Mofete Rosso is Palmento Costanzo's estate red: organically farmed Nerello Mascalese from the Santo Spirito contrada producing a fragrant, mineral-driven Etna Rosso with appealing freshness.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Contrada C (Chiappemacine)

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Contrada C Chiappemacine is among the lightest and most elegant of the Passopisciaro contrada series: fragrant, iron-tinged, with firm but fine-grained tannins.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Contrada R (Rampante)

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Contrada R Rampante, from the highest-altitude Passopisciaro site at 1,000 metres, produces the most structured and slow-evolving wine of the range: taut, mineral and built for exceptional longevity.

Cuvée page →

Passopisciaro Passorosso

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Passopisciaro (Vini Franchetti)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The entry-level Passopisciaro: blended from multiple north-slope plots, fragrant and approachable, offering a compelling introduction to the estate's Nerello Mascalese philosophy.

Cuvée page →

Pellegrino Moscato di Pantelleria

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Medium acidity · Refreshing · Festive

Cantine PellegrinoSicily€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

The Moscato di Pantelleria is a lighter, fresher sweet wine style compared to the passito: Zibibbo grapes not fully dried produce a wine of floral freshness, bright apricot character and lower alcohol, ideal as an aperitivo or light dessert wine.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Archineri Etna Bianco

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€€Still White

Archineri is Pietradolce's benchmark Etna Bianco Superiore: pure Carricante of nervy mineral tension, citrus brightness and the chalky volcanic finesse that defines Milo-area white wines.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Etna Rosso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Pietradolce's entry Etna Rosso: approachable, herb-scented Nerello Mascalese with cherry brightness and Etna's characteristic saline mineral finish.

Cuvée page →

Pietradolce Vigna Barbagalli

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Bold

PietradolceSicily€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Vigna Barbagalli is Pietradolce's prestige single-vineyard Etna Rosso from ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines at 850 metres: among the most collectible and age-worthy Nerello Mascalese produced on the north slope.

Cuvée page →

Planeta Cerasuolo di Vittoria

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Planeta Dorilli (Vittoria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Planeta's DOCG expression from the Dorilli contrada in Vittoria: classic cherry-sour-herb character of the appellation with the polish and consistency Planeta's winemaking team delivers across all estates.

Planeta Grillo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Savoury

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Planeta's varietal Grillo from the Menfi estate shows the Mediterranean coastal character of the grape: citrus verbena, sea-salt mineral and herbal freshness with the clean precision of Planeta's winemaking.

Cuvée page →

Planeta La Segreta Bianco

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still White

La Segreta Bianco blends native Grecanico with aromatic international varieties in Planeta's most accessible white: fresh, aromatic and immediately appealing with stone-fruit and citrus freshness.

Cuvée page →

Planeta La Segreta Rosso

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

PlanetaSicily€€€€€€Still Red

La Segreta Rosso is Planeta's multi-varietal entry red: Nero d'Avola blended with Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Franc for a smooth, approachable and consistent everyday Sicilian red with broad food versatility.

Planeta Sciaranuova Nerello Mascalese

★★★★★★★★★★

Planeta Sciaranuova (Etna)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Planeta's Etna Nerello Mascalese from the Sciaranuova estate in Solicchiata: the Planeta house style - expressive and accessible - applied to the volcanic north slope.

Tenuta RapitalaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Rapitala's varietal Catarratto from the Alcamo DOC shows the variety's almond-citrus freshness from high-altitude Camporeale vines with a cleaner, more mineral profile than coastal examples.

Serragghia Zibibbo Secco

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Funky · Contemplative

Serragghia (Pantelleria)Sicily€€€€€€€Still White

Serragghia's dry Zibibbo is among the most mineral and wild table wines produced in Sicily: zero-sulphur, biodynamic, from the volcanic pumice soils of Pantelleria, it captures the island's raw elemental character without sweetness.

Cuvée page →

Tasca d'Almerita Leone d'Almerita

★★★★★★★★★★

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Leone d'Almerita is the everyday entry point of the Tasca range: Catarratto and Grillo in a fresh, clean aromatic white for versatile food matching.

Tasca d'Almerita Nozze d'Oro

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€€Still White

Created for the fiftieth anniversary of Lucio and Franca Tasca's wedding, Nozze d'Oro pairs Inzolia's stone-fruit softness with Sauvignon Blanc's aromatic herbal freshness in an enduring premium Sicilian white.

Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Bianco

★★★★★★★★★★

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still White

Sicily's most widely distributed native-white blend: Regaleali Bianco is a reliable, aromatic white of citrus and stone fruit freshness from the island's historic Tasca estate.

Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Rosso

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tasca d'AlmeritaSicily€€€€€€Still Red

Regaleali Rosso has been Tasca's everyday red since 1968: a multi-varietal native blend of Nero d'Avola, Perricone and Nerello Mascalese showing the breadth of the Regaleali plateau's terroir.

Cuvée page →

Tascante Ghiaia Nera

★★★★★★★★★★

Tenuta Tascante (Tasca d'Almerita on Etna)Sicily€€€€€€€Still Red

Ghiaia Nera (black gravel) is the benchmark cuvee of Tasca's Etna estate, taking its name from the black volcanic gravel of the Rampante contrada: mineral-driven, fragrant Nerello Mascalese from the north slope.

Cuvée page →

Tascante Il Picciolo

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Tenuta Tascante (Tasca d'Almerita on Etna)Sicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Il Picciolo is Tascante's prestige single-vineyard Etna Rosso from old Nerello vines in a specific parcel within the Rampante contrada: the estate's most structured and complex expression.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Calderara Sottana

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Firm tannin · High acidity · Contemplative · Elegant

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Calderara Sottana is a benchmark for north-slope Etna Rosso: Burgundian elegance with volcanic minerality, tart red-fruit purity and the structural firmness that demands 8-plus years.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Etna Rosso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€Still Red

The estate Etna Rosso represents the breadth of Terre Nere's north-slope holdings: an approachable, everyday expression of Nerello Mascalese with cherry freshness and volcanic minerality.

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Feudo di Mezzo

★★★★★★★★★★

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Feudo di Mezzo is the most aromatic and perfumed of the Terre Nere lineup: complex, multi-layered, with chalky depth and remarkable persistence.

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Guardiola

★★★★★★★★★★

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

The Guardiola contrada yields the most powerful and structured of the Terre Nere single-vineyard range: darker fruit, greater density, and a long mineral-chalky finish.

Tenuta delle Terre Nere San Lorenzo

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tenuta delle Terre NereSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

San Lorenzo's lower-altitude vines yield a more accessible Terre Nere red: fragrant, iron-streaked, with vibrant sour-cherry acidity and a mineral iron finish.

Tenuta di Fessina 'A Puddara

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Tenuta di FessinaSicily€€€€€€€€Still White

From the Milo contrada entitled to the Etna Bianco Superiore designation, 'A Puddara (Sicilian for 'la pullera', hen with chicks) is Fessina's single-vineyard Carricante of pure mineral tension and volcanic chalk finesse.

Tenuta di Fessina Musmeci

★★★★★★★★★★

Tenuta di FessinaSicily€€€€€€€€Still Red

Musmeci is Fessina's prestige single-vineyard Etna Rosso from old Nerello Mascalese vines in the Rovittello contrada: structured, mineral-driven and among the north slope's most refined expressions.

Fondo Filara is the premium Nicosia Etna Rosso from organically farmed Nerello Mascalese on the south-east slope: approachable and mineral, reflecting the Trecastagni terroir.

★★★★★★★★★★

Monte Gorna is Nicosia's prestige Etna Rosso from a single contrada on the south-east slope: the estate's most structured and mineral wine, representing the full potential of the Trecastagni zone.

★★★★★★★★★★

Barone Ricasoli Castello di BrolioTuscany€€€€€€€Still Red

A traditionalist Chianti Classico honouring the 1872 Ricasoli formula: Sangiovese plus the rare Abrusco (Colorino-family) for colour and herbal lift, aged in large casks rather than barrique.

★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Azienda Agricola CesaniTuscany€€€€€€Still Red

Cesani's everyday Chianti from the Colli Senesi sub-zone south-west of San Gimignano. Field-blend Sangiovese with bright red-cherry, tomato-leaf and rosemary lift.

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Azienda Agricola CesaniTuscany€€€€€€€Still White

The intense Vernaccia from 40-plus-year-old Pancole vines. More yellow-fruit weight and saline length than the annata, with the indigenous almond signature.

Azienda Agricola CesaniTuscany€€€€€€Still White

★★★★★★★★★★

Marchesi AntinoriTuscany€€€€€€€Still Red

Sangiovese-led Chianti Classico Riserva in a juicier, more accessible Antinori register, with cherry and tobacco at the core.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Palazzo della Torre

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

A Veronese IGT using partial ripasso over Amarone pomace adding richness to the Corvina-Rondinella blend, while a small Sangiovese component adds acidity. Approachable but complex mid-tier red from Allegrini.

Cuvée page →

Allegrini Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

AllegriniVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Allegrini's estate Classico Superiore reflects the house's high Corvinone proportion (45%) adding floral lift and softer tannin alongside Corvina's cherry-herb character; a bright, food-friendly everyday Valpolicella.

Cuvée page →

Bertani Valpolicella Ripasso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

BertaniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Bertani's Ripasso benefits from the estate's traditional Corvina-Rondinella blend and refermentation over dried Amarone skins, producing a mid-weight red with the freshness typical of the traditional house style.

Cuvée page →

Ca' La Bionda Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Refreshing

Ca' La BiondaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Ca' La Bionda's Classico Superiore reflects the upper Marano valley's mineral character in a lighter-bodied, tart cherry register; crisp, food-friendly and genuinely typical of the traditional Valpolicella style.

Cuvée page →

Cantina di Custoza La Selva Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Cantina di CustozaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

La Selva is the cooperative's premium single-vineyard selection from older vines in the Selva parcel. Partial oak fermentation adds texture and complexity beyond the standard cooperative Custoza, demonstrating the appellation's premium potential.

Cuvée page →

Cavalchina Amedeo Bianco di Custoza DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium acidity · Elegant · Savoury

CavalchinaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Amedeo is Cavalchina's premium selection, using partial oak fermentation to add complexity and longevity to the Bianco di Custoza DOC framework. More structured and richer than the standard Custoza, with the oak well integrated.

Cuvée page →

Corte Gardoni Le Fontane Garda Cabernet DOC

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

Corte GardoniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

The Le Fontane (the springs) red from Corte Gardoni is a Garda DOC Cabernet-based blend aged in oak. The Garda DOC permits Bordeaux varieties and the morainic soils of Valeggio sul Mincio give the Cabernet a mineral freshness that distinguishes it from warmer Veneto reds.

Cuvée page →

Corte Sant'Alda Soraigo Valpolicella Superiore

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Funky · Rustic

Corte Sant'AldaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Soraigo shows what biodynamic Valpolicella Classico tastes like with minimal intervention: earthy, mineral, tart cherry with clay soil expressiveness and a savoury, food-friendly character that makes it a superb natural-wine discovery.

Cuvée page →

Dal Forno Romano Vigna Seré Passito Bianco

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

Dal Forno RomanoVeneto€€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

A rare white passito from Dal Forno, made from dried Moscato and Garganega grapes at the Illasi valley estate, applying the same concentration philosophy as the Amarone to a sweet white with orange blossom and apricot intensity.

Cuvée page →

Fasoli Gino Le Bine Soave Superiore DOCG

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Fasoli GinoVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Le Bine is the top single-vineyard Soave Superiore DOCG from Fasoli Gino's organically farmed estate. 'Bine' are the traditional wooden stakes used for old Garganega training; this label celebrates the estate's old-vine heritage from individual pergola-trained parcels on volcanic basalt.

Cuvée page →

Gini Col Foscarin Soave Classico

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Col Foscarin is Gini's barrel-aged prestige Soave from the Col Foscarin parcel in Monteforte: partial barrel-fermentation adds hazelnut cream and golden apple richness while the Garganega's mineral core remains crisp and long-finishing.

Cuvée page →

Gini Soave Classico La Frosca

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

GiniVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

La Frosca from the Gini family's oldest Garganega vines on the highest basalt-volcanic slopes of Monteforte d'Alpone; the chalky mineral texture, fennel herb and white-peach fruit represent the Classico zone's basalt terroir in its purest expression.

Cuvée page →

Giuseppe Quintarelli Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Giuseppe QuintarelliVeneto€€€€€€€€€Still Red

Quintarelli's entry-level Valpolicella is already a lesson in traditional winemaking: high-acid, savoury cherry fruit with dried-herb complexity and three years in large oak adding gentle structure.

Cuvée page →

Gorgo Merlot Garda DOC

4.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

GorgoVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Gorgo's Garda DOC Merlot provides a red wine counterpart to the estate's primary Bianco di Custoza focus. Lake Garda's moderating effect on the climate produces a well-rounded Merlot with soft tannins and ripe fruit.

Cuvée page →

Guerrieri Rizzardi Pojega Bardolino Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Guerrieri RizzardiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Pojega is the top expression from Guerrieri Rizzardi's Bardolino range, bottled under the Bardolino Superiore DOCG. Oak ageing adds structure and complexity to the base Classico profile. Named for the Pojega wine museum located on the estate.

Cuvée page →

Inama Bradisismo Soave Superiore DOCG

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Bradisismo (Italian for seismic land subsidence) is Inama's prestige selection from the oldest basalt-grown Garganega vines, aged 18-24 months in French barriques; arguably the most complex Soave available, with extraordinary tension between rich oak ageing and the grape's saline mineral core.

Cuvée page →

Inama Oratorio di San Lorenzo Soave Superiore DOCG

4.5
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Inama's Oratorio di San Lorenzo is the estate's second single-vineyard Soave Superiore from the adjacent San Lorenzo parcel, showing the same volcanic basalt minerality as Foscarino but with a slightly more herbal, fennel-accented character.

Cuvée page →

Inama Soave Classico Superiore Vigneti di Foscarino

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

Inama Azienda AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Vigneti di Foscarino is Inama's pure basalt-volcanic Soave Classico from the historic Foscarino hill, the most volcanically active terroir in the Classico zone; 100% Garganega barrel-fermented for a mineral, almond-hazelnut complexity of striking length.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Merlot Piave DOC

3.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Piave plain Merlot DOC from Italo Cescon is a classic Trevisan everyday red. Merlot on the alluvial Piave soils produces a plum-forward, medium-bodied wine with soft tannins suited to simple food pairings.

Cuvée page →

Italo Cescon Raboso Piave DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Bold

Italo CesconVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Raboso Piave is one of Italy's most ancient and austere red grapes, native to the Piave river plains in Treviso province. Italo Cescon has championed its rehabilitation since the 1960s. High tannin and acid give the wine its characteristic austerity, but serious age-worthiness.

Cuvée page →

Le Salette Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Le SaletteVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Le Salette's Classico Superiore benefits from the high-altitude Fumane terroir that gives freshness and floral lift to the Corvina base; an elegant, food-friendly expression of the appellation.

Cuvée page →

Lorenzo Begali Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Lorenzo BegaliVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Begali's Valpolicella Classico Superiore from Ca' Bianca old vines shows the same mineral-iron freshness as the estate's Amarone in a lighter register; a food-friendly expression of traditional San Pietro in Cariano Corvina.

Cuvée page →

Masi Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · Medium acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Masi AgricolaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Campofiorin pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964, passing fresh Valpolicella wine over dried Amarone grape skins to extract colour, richness and complexity. A seminal wine that created an entire style category.

Cuvée page →

Monte Tondo Miele Recioto di Soave DOCG

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Festive · Elegant

Monte TondoVeneto€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Miele (honey) is Monte Tondo's Recioto di Soave DOCG, a rare sweet wine from appassimento-dried Garganega grapes. Recioto di Soave predates Soave's dry style and is considered the original historic wine of the Soave hills. Monte Tondo's version captures honey, dried apricot and candied citrus balanced by the grape's naturally high acidity.

Cuvée page →

Monte Tondo Soave Superiore Casette Foscarin

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

Monte TondoVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Monte Tondo's signature Soave Superiore DOCG from the Casette Foscarin single-vineyard site on high-elevation basalt terraces east of the Classico zone. Extended lees contact adds texture while preserving the natural mineral character of volcanic Garganega.

Cuvée page →

Nicolis Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Nicolis Angelo e FigliVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

The Nicolis estate Classico Superiore offers an honest, traditional Valpolicella from the Villa Girardi hillside; cherry-tobacco character with the freshness typical of a classic-zone Corvina aged gently in large casks.

Cuvée page →

Ottella Le Creete Lugana DOC

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Elegant · Savoury

OttellaVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Le Creete (meaning 'the clay/chalk') is named for the specific Cretaceous clay-limestone soils of the parcel. An intermediate tier between the standard Lugana and the full Riserva Molceo; extended stainless steel lees contact adds texture without the oak of the Riserva.

Cuvée page →

Ottella Lugana DOC

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · High acidity · Refreshing · Easygoing

OttellaVeneto€€€€€€Still White

Ottella's standard Lugana DOC is produced from Turbiana (the local name for Verdicchio in the Lugana DOC zone) from estate vineyards at Peschiera del Garda on the southeastern shore of Lake Garda. The clay-limestone soils of the Lugana plain give Turbiana its distinctive almond mineral character.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Le Colombare Recioto di Soave

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Romantic · Elegant

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Pieropan's Recioto di Soave from the Le Colombare parcel is the most prestigious expression of the ancient sweet white wine tradition of the Soave zone; dried Garganega produces extraordinary apricot-honey concentration kept fresh by the varietal's trademark high acidity.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico Calvarino

4.9
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Calvarino is Pieropan's basalt and limestone single-vineyard Soave, planted in 1924 to Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave; the volcanic basalt soils give a crackling acidity and mineral depth far beyond the appellation average, with exceptional ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Pieropan Soave Classico La Rocca

5.0
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PieropanVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

La Rocca is 100% Garganega from the volcanic tufa hillside above Soave castle, barrel-fermented and aged; arguably Italy's most cellar-worthy white wine, developing hazelnut-honey complexity over 10-plus years while retaining the grape's almond-mineral core.

Cuvée page →

Pra Monte Grande Soave Classico

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

PraVeneto€€€€€€€Still White

Pra's Monte Grande from the basalt-rich Monteforte hillside is a benchmark organic Soave Classico: Graziano Pra's organic viticulture and extended lees contact produce a pure, mineral, textural wine of genuine complexity and age-worthiness for the price.

Cuvée page →

Pra Otto Soave Classico

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

PraVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Otto is Pra's barrel-aged prestige Soave, named for the year the oldest vines were planted (1908); extensive lees contact in barriques produces a Garganega of remarkable richness, hazelnut-cream complexity and long ageing potential.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi Capitel Croce

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Elegant · Contemplative

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Capitel Croce is Anselmi's barrel-aged 100% Garganega, named for a wayside cross on the Capitel Croce hill; oak-fermented with extended lees contact to produce a richer, more complex expression of the varietal with hazelnuts, golden apple and creamy texture.

Cuvée page →

Roberto Anselmi I Capitelli

4.7
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

Roberto AnselmiVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Late Harvest

I Capitelli is Anselmi's late-harvest sweet Garganega, made from overripe and botrytised bunches from the Capitel Croce and Capitel Foscarino hills; rich, honeyed and apricot-dominated but lifted by the grape's natural acidity that keeps it from cloying.

Cuvée page →

Santa Sofia Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Santa SofiaVeneto€€€€€€Still Red

Santa Sofia's Classico Superiore is a reliable, food-friendly, accessible Valpolicella from the historic Pedemonte estate with cherry-tobacco character and good acidity.

Cuvée page →

Serafini and Vidotto Phigaia

4.1
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Easygoing · Rustic

Serafini and VidottoVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Phigaia is the accessible second wine from Serafini and Vidotto, allowing the estate to express the Montello terroir at a lower price point. The Cabernet Franc and Merlot blend is lighter, fresher and more immediately enjoyable than Il Rosso dell'Abazia.

Cuvée page →

Speri Valpolicella Classico Superiore La Roverina

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Speri ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Speri's La Roverina Classico Superiore with partial ripasso technique; fresh cherry and tobacco character from the traditional large-cask approach, with added concentration from the ripasso step and the estate's characteristic acidity.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Amedeo Recioto di Soave

4.6
★★★★★★★★★★

Sweet · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Romantic · Contemplative

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€€Dessert Passito

Amedeo is Suavia's Recioto di Soave DOCG from the ancient Monte Carbonare vineyard; the sweet dried-Garganega wine balances luscious apricot-honey sweetness with the grape's natural high acidity, keeping the finish fresh and complex.

Cuvée page →

Suavia Soave Classico Monte Carbonare

4.8
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · Racy acidity · Elegant · Refreshing

SuaviaVeneto€€€€€€€€Still White

Suavia's Monte Carbonare from the village of Soave itself: 100% Garganega from a steep volcanic tufa hillside with century-old vines; lees-aged in large casks producing exceptional mineral depth, citrus-almond precision and great cellaring potential.

Cuvée page →

Tedeschi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Capitel dei Nicalo

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tedeschi ViniVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

The Capitel dei Nicalo is Tedeschi's Valpolicella Superiore partially ripassoed over dried skins, offering accessible Corvina cherry fruit with tobacco structure and good food-pairing versatility.

Cuvée page →

Tenuta Sant'Antonio Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tenuta Sant'AntonioVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Tenuta Sant'Antonio's Ripasso shows the Mezzane valley's characteristic earthiness alongside cherry-tobacco fruit; the small Oseleta addition adds a distinguishing spice note that sets it apart from Classico-zone examples.

Cuvée page →

Tommasi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

Tommasi ViticoltoriVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Tommasi's Ripasso offers the hallmark refermentation complexity over dried Amarone skins in a well-structured, cherry-tobacco dominated medium red that is one of the zone's best-value offerings in the Ripasso DOC.

Cuvée page →

Valentina Cubi Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.3
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Funky

Valentina CubiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Valentina Cubi's organic Valpolicella Superiore shows the freshness of high-altitude Fumane fruit with earthy mineral character from organic farming; a genuinely characterful everyday Valpolicella with better-than-average expressiveness.

Cuvée page →

Vignalta Cabernet Colli Euganei Rosso

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Savoury · Rustic

VignaltaVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

A Cabernet-focused red from the volcanic slopes of the Euganean Hills. The Colli Euganei DOC allows Cabernet varieties and Vignalta's version shows the distinctive mineral thread of volcanic soil combined with the classic Cabernet Franc herbal character.

Cuvée page →

Viviani Valpolicella Classico Superiore

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Refreshing

VivianiVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Viviani's Classico Superiore from the high Mazzano ridge shows the altitude freshness that characterises all the estate's wines: iron mineral, tart cherry and mountain herbs in a lean, food-loving medium-red.

Cuvée page →

Zenato Valpolicella Superiore Ripassa

4.4
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Medium tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Easygoing

ZenatoVeneto€€€€€€€Still Red

Zenato's Ripassa shows the Oseleta grape's peppery-spice influence even at the mid-tier level: good concentration from refermentation over dried skins with a distinguishing warmth from the native Oseleta component.

Grüner Veltliner Smaragd Wösendorf

4.2
★★★★★★★★★★

Dry · Medium-bodied · Low tannin · High acidity · Rustic · Savoury

Weingut Erich MachherndlWachau€€€€€€€Still White

Traditional-style Grüner Veltliner Smaragd from the 1786-founded Machherndl estate at Wösendorf; honest pepper-and-pear character aged in old barrels.
